acceptance_criteria: The experiment can compare at least: same base rate with different k2, same k2 with different base rate, and the current combined fragile/resilient comparison.
Outputs report crisis incidence or time-to-crisis in a way that isolates the contribution of each parameter change.
Any new prose recommendation clearly states whether the current section can be strengthened or should remain as-is.
Tests cover the new comparison surface without encoding narrative conclusions as tautologies.
constraints: This is a validity-improving simulation task, not a license to broaden the blog claim beyond what the results support.
Avoid overwriting the accepted evidence chain until the new results are reviewed.
description: Extend the fragility_regimes experiment so future prose can distinguish what comes from debt-drag sensitivity, what comes from base-rate level changes, and what comes from initial leverage. Produce an interpretable comparison or note if the existing framing should stay narrow.
goal: Strengthen the validity of the fragility section by separating leverage-sensitivity effects from base-rate effects in the Keen-based regime comparison.
implementation_notes: Superseded by task-019 and task-021, which place fragility inside the broader agent-based policy-controller comparison and final artifact bundle rather than as a standalone board lane.
title: Fragility robustness and parameter-isolation sweep
